name: Natural Park of the Hoces del Cabriel — Circular via Fuente La Oliva Trail (PR-CV 344)
name_original: Parque Natural de las Hoces del Cabriel — Circular por Sendero de Fuente La Oliva (PR-CV 344)
region_intro: Hiking around Venta Del Moro offers access to diverse natural landscapes, primarily within the Natural Park of the Hoces del Cabriel. The region is characterized by dramatic geological formations, including impressive rock formations like "Los Cuchillos de Contreras" and deep gorges carved by the Cabriel River. The terrain also features the rugged Sierra del Rubial and extends into the vineyard-rich La Plana de Utiel-Requena area. These varied features provide a range of environments for outdoor exploration. Best hiking trails around Venta Del Moro The most popular hiking route is Cuchillos de Contreras Trail, a 3.7 miles (6.0 km) trail that takes 1 hour 47 minutes to complete. This easy route leads through the Parque Natural de las Hoces del Cabriel, showcasing the notable Los Cuchillos formations. Another top favourite among local hikers is Los Cuchillos del Cabriel Trail, an easy 4.0 miles (6.4 km) path. This trail offers views of the Cabriel River's sculpted landscape and takes approximately 1 hour 42 minutes. Local hikers also love the Natural Park of the Hoces del Cabriel — Circular via Fuente La Oliva Trail (PR-CV 344), an 8.7 miles (14.0 km) trail leading through the heart of the natural park, often completed in about 4 hours 14 minutes. Hiking around Venta Del Moro is defined by the deep gorges of the Cabriel River, dramatic rock formations, and extensive vineyards. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult routes, catering to various ability levels. The routes in Venta Del Moro are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 50 reviews. More than 350 hikers have used komoot to explore Venta Del Moro's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Venta Del Moro?
A: Venta Del Moro offers a wide selection of hiking opportunities, with over 35 trails to explore. These routes cater to various skill levels, including 14 easy, 19 moderate, and 4 difficult options.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Venta Del Moro?
A: The region is enjoyable for hiking throughout much of the year. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures, ideal for exploring the natural landscapes. Summer can be warm, but trails along the Cabriel River or in shaded areas provide cooler options. Winter hikes are also possible, offering a different perspective of the dramatic geological formations.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see while hiking in Venta Del Moro?
A: Hiking in Venta Del Moro allows you to discover stunning natural features, particularly within the Natural Park of the Hoces del Cabriel. You can witness the impressive rock formations known as "Los Cuchillos de Contreras" and the deep gorges carved by the Cabriel River. Notable viewpoints include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/812619" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hoz del Rabo de la Sartén Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4602504" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Fonseca Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;, offering panoramic views of the landscape.

Q: Are there easy hiking trails suitable for beginners in Venta Del Moro?
A: Yes, Venta Del Moro has 14 easy hiking trails perfect for beginners or those seeking a relaxed walk. An excellent option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31429379" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñas Blancas Path Trench – Peñas Blancas Viewpoint loop from Trinxeras de Contreras&lt;/a&gt;, which is 4.9 km long and offers scenic views without significant elevation gain.

Q: Can I find challenging hikes in Venta Del Moro?
A: For experienced hikers looking for a challenge, Venta Del Moro offers 4 difficult routes. One such demanding trail is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14709132" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cuchillares de Contreras – La Fonseca Viewpoint loop from Casas Mirasol&lt;/a&gt;, which spans 21.2 km with substantial elevation changes, providing a rigorous and rewarding experience.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Venta Del Moro?
A: Yes, several circular routes are available, allowing you to start and end your hike at the same point. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e823652716/natural-park-of-the-hoces-del-cabriel-circular-via-fuente-la-oliva-trail-pr-cv-344" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Natural Park of the Hoces del Cabriel — Circular via Fuente La Oliva Trail (PR-CV 344)&lt;/a&gt; is a popular moderate option, covering 14.0 km and immersing you in the heart of the natural park.

Q: Is Venta Del Moro suitable for family hikes?
A: Absolutely. With 14 easy trails, Venta Del Moro is well-suited for family outings. Many routes feature gentle inclines and manageable distances, allowing families to enjoy the natural beauty of the Hoces del Cabriel Natural Park together. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1012728040/los-cuchillos-del-cabriel-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Cuchillos del Cabriel Trail&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 6.4 km path that families can enjoy.

Q: Are dogs allowed on the hiking trails in Venta Del Moro?
A: While specific regulations can vary by protected area, generally, dogs are welcome on many trails in Venta Del Moro, especially if kept on a leash. It's always advisable to check local signage within the Natural Park of the Hoces del Cabriel for any specific restrictions or protected wildlife areas.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Venta Del Moro?
A: The trails in Venta Del Moro are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.7 stars from over 50 reviews. Hikers frequently praise the stunning geological formations, the beauty of the Cabriel River, and the diverse landscapes that offer something for every fitness level.

Q: Are there any historical sites or points of interest along the trails?
A: Yes, beyond natural beauty, you can encounter historical points of interest. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4602462" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñas Blancas Path Trench&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3867155" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Contreras Trench&lt;/a&gt; are notable historical sites. Additionally, impressive structures like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4475727" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vadocañas Bridge&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4475652" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Contreras Bridge - Cabriel River&lt;/a&gt; are often found along or near hiking routes, adding a cultural dimension to your outdoor adventure.

Q: Can I find trails that follow the Cabriel River?
A: The Cabriel River is a central feature of the region, carving spectacular ravines and gorges. Many trails offer views of or run alongside the river. Segments of the Turia-Cabriel Nature Trail, such as the section near the Vadocañas bridge, provide excellent opportunities to hike along the river, often on gravel paths with gentle inclines.
tips: One of the panoramic points of the Fuente de Oliva route with beautiful views of the Cabriel river valley and the Hoces del Cabriel Natural Park with its striking "cuchillares"
Located in the Hoces del Cabriel Nature Reserve, not far from the Vicente viewpoint.

waypoints.name: Hoz del Rabo de la Sartén Viewpoint
